figures.caption: FIG. 5: Local SHAP explanations and counterfactual partial-dependence analysis. Figure (a) shows local contributions for VPt8 and LiIr; figure (b) shows three instances where the model underestimates SHC by more than 900 (ℏ/e)(S/cm) on the training set - all Pt-free heavy-element compositions whose residual is dominated by the pfrac and ⟨p⟩ features. Figure (c) shows the effect of pfrac under the presence of Pt, the combined interaction shows the massive effect that pfrac has on the predicted SHC. Figure (d) shows the partial-dependence on a subset containing heavy element compositions and figure (e) shows the same effect after deflating the statistical entanglement between the features Pt and pfrac. Removing the dependence of pfrac by counterfactual interrogation tones the effect down significantly with the gradient dropping from 3.7 (figure (d)) to 0.53 (figure (e)) in boxcox-transformed units, illustrating that the model has learned to associate Pt content with enhanced SHC; held-out evaluation (table (I)) shows that this association translates to a measurable but regime-dependent screening consequence.

FIG. 7: Figure (a) shows the two most significant principal components of the SHAP-transformed dataset. Materials with reported SHC values above 2000 (ℏ/e)(S/cm) (orange dots) cluster in a distinct region of the SHAP-PC space, indicating that the model has learned a coherent compositional pattern characteristic of high-SHC entries in the training set. Figures (b) and (c) decompose the same projection by the ⟨f⟩ and pfrac feature values, respectively, showing that the cluster is driven by these two features in combination. SHC in figure (a) is in Box-Cox-transformed units.

FIG. 8: Orbital-projected density of states (PDOS) for the four heavy-element validation compounds, with energy referenced to the Fermi level. (a) VPt8: dense Pt 5d manifold at EF drives the confirmed high SHC. (b) LiIr: Ir 5d states dominate while Li is an electronic spectator. (c) HgOsPb2: Os 5d states dominate at EF , the origin of the DFT-confirmed 2703 (ℏ/e)(S/cm) peak that the composition-only model under-predicts by ∼ 3.8×. (d) HfC: a low total DOS at EF with no dense d-manifold explains the small DFT SHC and the model’s over-prediction. Energy window shown is ±6 eV about EF .

TABLE II: First-principles validation of model predictions. SHC values are the maximum absolute component |σz xy|; the full-window peak is compared against the model (consistent with the training-label convention), and the near-EF value
